rationale.R1.5: The request to clean up the passage centrally includes improving the section-level flow and ordering of the evaluation framing, protocol, results, and contribution claims.
rationale.R1.6: Cleaning up a research passage most directly requires sentence- and paragraph-level improvements in clarity, concision, flow, and academic register.
rationale.RX.1: Any cleanup should preserve uncertainty around BLEU-based reproducibility and avoid making unsupported precision claims.
rationale.RX.2: The artifact contains potentially strong claims such as an upper bound and direct novelty, so avoiding overstatement is an active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The delegation asks only for cleanup, so the work must stay within improving presentation rather than conducting new analysis or evaluation.
rationale.RX.4: Because the text concerns reproducibility of analytical assistants, revisions should avoid language that could mislead downstream methodological interpretation.
rationale.RX.5: Cleanup should preserve traceability to the reported protocol details, metrics, table references, and contribution framing already present in the artifact.
